response:
{
  "summary": "PEP 8 is the official style guide for Python code. It provides detailed conventions for writing readable, consistent Python code, including indentation, line length, line breaks, whitespace, naming conventions, comments, docstrings, imports, and programming recommendations. Following PEP 8 helps make Python code easier to read and maintain across projects and teams.",
  "key_facts": [
    "Use 4 spaces per indentation level and avoid mixing tabs and spaces.",
    "Limit lines to a maximum of 79 characters for code and 72 for docstrings/comments.",
    "Surround top-level functions and classes with two blank lines; surround method definitions inside classes with one blank line.",
    "Use spaces around operators and after commas, but avoid extraneous whitespace inside parentheses, brackets, or braces.",
    "Follow consistent naming conventions: functions/variables in snake_case, classes in CapWords (CamelCase), constants in UPPER_CASE.",
    "Write docstrings for all public modules, functions, classes, and methods; use triple-quoted strings.",
    "Organize imports: standard library, related third-party, local application/library, each in separate groups and usually in alphabetical order.",
    "Avoid wildcard imports; prefer explicit imports or use explicit relative imports when appropriate.",
    "Break lines before binary operators in long expressions, following Knuth's style for readability.",
    "Use trailing commas in multi-line collections/function calls to simplify future diffs when the list is extended."
  ],
  "relevant_quotes": [
    "Imports are always put at the top of the file, just after any module comments and docstrings, and before module globals and constants.",
    "Wildcard imports (from <module> import *) should be avoided, as they make it unclear which names are present in the namespace, confusing both readers and many automated tools.",
    "In Python code, it is permissible to break before or after a binary operator, as long as the convention is consistent locally. For new code Knuth's style is suggested.",
    "Use the function naming rules: lowercase with words separated by underscores as necessary to improve readability.",
    "For one liner docstrings, please keep the closing \"\"\" on the same line."
  ]
}
